title: Integrated Anatomical and Physiological Sciences Part B

prerequisites.text: HUB2019F (or approved equivalent) and CEM1000W (or approved equivalent).

outline: The course integrates aspects of human physiology, anatomy and histology of organ systems including cardiovascular, respiratory, nervous, reproductive, urinary and immune systems. Homeostasis and regulation form the golden thread throughout the course. Homeostatic concepts covered include thermoregulation, acid-base balance, neural transduction, cardiac output and regulation, and respiration. Students are also introduced to anthropology and concepts of ageing and disease. Practicals involve studying the physiology of the nervous system, the electrical events in the contraction of cardiac muscle and the mechanics of the respiratory system, as well as examining human anatomical specimens and histology of the organ systems.
lecture_times: Lectures: Monday to Friday, 08h00–08h45. Practicals: Mondays or Tuesdays, 14h00–17h00.
dp_requirements: Attendance at all practicals; 40% average in class tests; and an average of 50% for all assignments.
assessment: Class tests 30%; practicals, assignments and tutorials 20%; final examinations 50% (written theory 30% + practical 20%). Sub-minimum of 40% required for both theory and practical examinations. Supplementary examinations may be offered to students with an overall score of 45–49%. An oral examination may be required for selected students.
